builders.name: Aaron Levie
builders.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

tweets.text:
We will likely have more lawyers in the future than today, because:

1) AI will cause so many more people to ask legal questions which will encourage them to need to verify or execute through an actual lawyer. 

2) AI will cause an explosion of more and more exotic legal terms that lawyers will be spending even more time reviewing redlines or new cases around.

3) All the new areas of law that now are emerging around the use of AI itself in every single industry. AI introduces an explosion of IP, privacy, and regulatory compliance challenges across all verticals.

This has historical precedent as well. Between the creation of the PC and the internet (both technologies that made the legal profession far more efficient), the ABA pegs active attorneys having gone from roughly 400,000 in 1975 to roughly 1,375,000 in 2025.

When we make professions more efficient and automated, often demand for them goes up not down.

tweets.text:
Don't just ask AI to "summarize" a piece of long-form/difficult content. Try these prompts:

1. Remix it into a polished magazine article, preserving the best original quotes
2. Remix it into a Socratic dialogue between a teacher & a student (representing me)
3. Based on what you know about me, pick out the insights that I would find the most interesting

tweets.text:
VCs: please take this low valuation since I’m investing in you and my conviction is worth the price. I’m invested for the long long term. 

6 months later..

Same VC: take the highest possible valuation because why not. Who cares about the partner or fund name. Less dilution is good. More money is good. (Also it’ll help me show marks to LPs so take it bro please).  

Try to avoid these VCs like the plague. All they care about is their “deals” getting markups. I get it - generating returns is the job. 

But, watch these people also run away when the company is not doing well. All those intros and goodwill suddenly disappear. 

Try to get missionaries over mercenaries. And please please please backchannel the person who’ll be joining your cap table!

builders.name: Peter Steinberger
builders.bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞

tweets.text:
Harnesses can now been plugins - meaning it'd be trivial to replace the default Pi harness with e.g. Anthropic’s SDK or another custom one.

This won't magically allow Claude subscription, but it's useful to compare different implementations.

Using codex as harness will likely weaken personality, but should increase agentic mode to keep the agent working on longer-horizon tasks.

So far I gated strict mode to GPT since it's not yet tested with other models, but it's trivial to modify that if you use hackable install.

2) Native Codex as the OpenClaw harness:

plugins.entries.codex.enabled = true
agents.defaults.model = "codex/gpt-5.4"
agents.defaults.embeddedHarness = { runtime: "codex", fallback: "none" }

Codex owns threads, resume, compaction, app-server execution. https://t.co/9DDpY6TMqf

Two experiments in the next @openclaw to address some "GPT is lazy" issues: 
1) Strict mode:

agents.defaults.embeddedPi.executionContract = "strict-agentic"

This tells GPT-5.x to keep working: read more code, call tools, make changes, or return a real blocker instead of stopping at “here’s the plan.” https://t.co/QMdeus6Y5J

tweets.text: blockchain capital dumped their entire 216,292 AAVE position for $24.8m in march. parafi sold too. stani kulechov responded by personally buying $16m worth across december and february. token down 64% from highs to $94. protocol just did $142m annualized revenue, V4 hit $10b deposits in 10 days with zero incentives, 95% of supply already unlocked so no vesting cliff ahead. VCs are liquidity. founders are conviction. when the guy who built it is buying what the money managers are dumping, you pay attention to the guy who built it.

prop AMMs (tessera, elfomofi, wasabi) just processed $1.68b in 30 days on base, hit 22% of DEX volume in a single day. aerodrome's ve(3,3) model needs volume to generate fees to attract bribes to lock veAERO to deepen liquidity. remove 10-15% of that volume permanently and the flywheel doesn't spin the same way. AERO is priced for base DEX monopoly. these three protocols just proved that's no longer guaranteed. the negative reflexivity of a ve(3,3) model losing volume share is something most holders haven't stress tested

kamino crossed $1b in RWA lending and now controls 52% of all RWA deposits across DeFi lending protocols. not on ethereum. on solana. during a quarter where SOL dropped 36% and drift got exploited for $285m. aave trades at $2b+ FDV, morpho at $973m. kamino owns majority market share in the only DeFi vertical that connects to $130t in traditional fixed income and probably trades below both. the concentration risk question matters though. if this is 5 whales it's centrifuge with extra steps. if TVL holds above $1b through may after the drift fallout, the thesis is confirmed and the valuation gap closes hard.

builders.bio: searching for patterns | AI @limitlessFT | @26cryptocapital | Prev. @coinbase @consensys

tweets.text:
man what a week

> anthropic announces mythos can hack anything, can’t release publicly

> china then reveals their state database got hacked “for months” (mythos was complete feb 24th 🙂)

> mexican government just hacked by someone using claude + chatgpt. leaked 2 million private records. 

> openai confirmed releasing cybersecurity AGI in a few months 

> someone throws a fucking molotov cocktail at sam altmans house protesting, gets arrested.

> 2 heads of global finance Bessent and Powell start panicking, run an emergency meeting with banks to lock
their shit down ahead of mythos release

> meanwhile software stocks are crashing as claude automates their moat

> meta and anthropic sign major deals with corweave, compute is all that matters now. stock up 30% on the news

yeah, we’ll look back on 2026 as the year we hit AGI

the fear around AI is overblown

something that makes me certain AI isn’t going to replace us is non-determinism:

ai and robots are (for better or worse) deterministic creatures. 

no matter how “human” they appear - there’s always a binary(ish) outcome. 

humans are built different. you never know our next move or what we’re thinking.

that gives us a sense of hope that theres a way (however small) that we can create the reality we desire

and i think that’s fucking beautiful 🤷🏽
my 5-layer SPV investment looking really fucking good right now https://t.co/4gNkyL2moM

tweets.text:
You studied the charts. 
Watched the interviews. 
Read the whitepapers. 
Did everything a "responsible trader" is supposed to do.

And you still got REKT.
Nothing went wrong. 
The game worked exactly as it was designed to.
90-95% of retail traders lose money. 
The industry counts on it. 
Your losses are their revenue.

The only way to escape is to learn the Mechanical Rules and stop playing their games.
The most dangerous sentence in trading is "just this once." Every blown account in history started with someone making an exception to their own rules.
